meta.title: A205 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes A205 engine — M274 and M264 petrols, OM651 and OM654 diesels, and the M177 AMG V8. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Compare prices from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes C-Class A205 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned A205 engine typically costs £2,200–£15,000 supply only [MB-QUOTE], depending on engine — the volume OM654 diesel sits at £3,000–£5,000. Fitting adds 10–20 hours labour at specialist rates due to the cabriolet‑specific engine bay packaging. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes A205 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM654.920 (C220d facelift, 2018–2023) — a 2.0‑litre four‑cylinder diesel with improved timing chain design and significantly better reliability than the OM651 it replaced. The M264 E20 DEH LA (C300 facelift) is the best petrol option — 258 bhp with 48V mild‑hybrid assistance and fewer reported issues than the pre‑facelift M274.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes C-Class A205?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM651 rear timing chain failure (pre‑facelift diesel), M274 timing chain and fuel pump failure (pre‑facelift petrol), M264 valve and cooling system issues (facelift petrol), and — chassis‑specific — the cabriolet roof mechanism and rear‑window seal delamination on all cars.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes C-Class A205 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on which engine and whether you buy pre‑facelift (2016–2018) or facelift (2018–2023). The facelift M264 petrol and OM654 diesel are significantly more reliable than the pre‑facelift M274 and OM651. The M177 AMG V8 is robust but carries high running costs. The A205's real reliability challenge is the cabriolet roof mechanism, not the engines themselves.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes A205 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but a pre‑facelift OM651 (C220d/C250d, 2016–2018) without documented timing chain replacement is the one we'd approach cautiously, given the rear‑chain design and £1,500+ replacement cost. A neglected pre‑facelift M274 without timing chain or fuel pump history is the second.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my A205 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold‑start rattle (OM651 or M274 timing chain), long cranking or loss of power (M274 fuel pump), misfire or EML (M264 valve/cooling issues), white smoke or oil leaks (M177 rear‑main seal). Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes C-Class A205 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £20,000 A205, a £3,000–£5,000 OM654 recon is 15–25% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £15,000 pre‑facelift example, the same recon is 20–33% — still safe. Against a £15,000 AMG with an £8,000–£15,000 M177 recon, that's 53–100% — evaluate carefully. Our 70% Mercedes threshold is the line.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes A205 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM654 diesel can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing. The M264 petrol can reach 150,000+ miles with valve and cooling system attention. The M177 V8 is robust but demands meticulous oil changes and specialist maintenance — 100,000+ miles is achievable with care.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the A205 and the C205 C-Class?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both share the fourth‑generation C‑Class platform and most engines. The A205 is the 2‑door cabriolet (this page), the C205 is the 2‑door coupé. The cabriolet has additional structural bracing, a heavier kerb weight, and a more complex roof mechanism — costs on the A205 are typically slightly higher than the C205 for comparable engine work.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes A205 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and vehicle documentation; also stamped on the engine block (visible from below — the M264 is at the front, OM651/OM654 on the side). Tell us your variant, registration year and pre‑ or post‑facelift status and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes C-Class A205 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Mercedes C-Class A205 cabriolet — from the 1.6-litre M274 turbo petrol to the 510bhp AMG V8, plus the OM651 and OM654 diesels that power the UK's favourite open-top executive. Real UK replacement costs, honest reliability data, and the 2018 facelift that brought 48V mild-hybrid tech to the range. Backed by 276+ real UK A205 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes C-Class A205 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the A205 cabriolet specifically — from the pre‑facelift M274 and OM651 units to the facelift M264 and OM654 families, plus the M177 V8 that powers the C63 AMG.

258 bhp (190 kW) + 14 bhp (10 kW) EQ Boost

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All ten engine families are in Case B — no dedicated Engine Page exists yet for any Mercedes M274, M264, OM651, OM654 or M177 family. Ratings are derived from the Known Failure Patterns table in EM_Proprietary_Data and cross‑referenced against specialist/owner sources. The M264 and OM654 are considered more reliable than their predecessors; the M177 V8 is robust but carries high running costs. All ratings are provisional pending dedicated Engine Pages.

overview.intro: The A205 is the cabriolet body of Mercedes‑Benz's fourth‑generation C‑Class, launched at the 2016 Geneva Motor Show as the open‑top companion to the W205 saloon and C205 coupé. Built on the lightweight MRA platform, it offered a fully electric fabric roof that could be opened or closed in under 20 seconds at speeds up to 30 mph. A significant 2018 facelift brought new powertrains: the M274 petrol and OM651 diesel were replaced by the more efficient M264 (with 48V mild‑hybrid EQ Boost) and OM654 families. At the top of the range sat the AMG C63 — a 510bhp V8‑powered cabriolet that could hit 62 mph in 4.1 seconds with the roof down.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes MRA (Modular Rear Architecture) • Predecessor: A204 (C‑Class cabriolet) • Successor: A236 (CLE Cabriolet) • Sibling body styles in this generation: W205 (saloon), S205 (estate), C205 (coupé)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The A205 cabriolet generated an estimated 276+ UK engine enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED — PLACEHOLDER], concentrated on the C220d diesel as these premium cabriolets enter affordable second‑hand ownership.

items.quote: The 2.0‑litre four‑cylinder diesel — 194 bhp, 400 Nm of torque, and a significant step forward from the OM651 it replaced. Quieter, smoother, and more efficient, with a timing chain that's more durable than the OM651's notorious rear‑mounted unit.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want modern diesel refinement without the OM651's chain concerns.

items.quote: The 2.0‑litre turbo petrol with 48V mild‑hybrid assistance — 258 bhp plus 14 bhp EQ Boost, 370 Nm of torque, and the seamless stop‑start that only a 48V system can deliver. It's the sweet spot of the petrol range: more powerful than the C200, but without the AMG's running costs.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six‑cylinder performance from a four‑cylinder package with modern hybrid efficiency.

items.quote: The 2.1‑litre twin‑turbo diesel — timing chain at the rear of the engine, a £2,500+ job when it fails, and a design that was already ageing by the time the A205 launched in 2016. Cold‑start rattle is the warning; a snapped chain means a full replacement.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who understand the OM651's reputation and budget for chain replacement — or buy the facelift OM654 instead.

items.quote: The 4.0‑litre twin‑turbo V8 is a masterpiece, but when it fails — hot‑vee design, valve stem seals, timing chain, or the rear‑main seal leak that affects many M177 units — the repair bills are in a different league. Low production numbers (cabriolet AMG is rare) mean parts are specialist‑only.
items.whoItsFor: collectors and enthusiasts who understand that 510 bhp comes with a six‑figure maintenance budget.

items.quote: The 1.5‑litre turbo with 48V mild hybrid — 184 bhp plus 14 bhp EQ Boost, 280 Nm of torque, and the most efficient petrol in the range. It's the entry‑point to the A205 range, but the 48V system makes it feel more refined than its displacement suggests.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want modern petrol efficiency without the C300's higher running costs.

items.quote: The facelift C300 is often overshadowed by the AMG models and the diesel C220d, but it's the quiet achiever of the range — 258 bhp, 48V mild hybrid, and none of the OM651's chain concerns or the M177's running costs.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want near‑AMG performance with four‑cylinder running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Watch — pre‑facelift M274 has known issues
Highest Risk — rear chain replacement is a big job
Best Petrol — 48V mild hybrid adds efficiency

economicsRule.title: The A205 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,000–£5,000 OM654 recon against a £15,000–£35,000 A205 sits at 8–33% of the car's value — a repair decision, not a financial one. Even the £8,000–£15,000 M177 AMG recon sits at 23–100% against a £15,000 AMG — on a sub‑£20,000 AMG cabriolet, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold. Value the car first, and always budget for the cabriolet roof mechanism's maintenance too.

keyTakeaways.question: Which A205 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M264 E15 DEH LA (C200 facelift) — efficient, 48V mild hybrid, low running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which A205 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M177 DE40 LA (C63 S) — V8 running costs, AMG parts prices, and cabriolet-specific maintenance.

keyTakeaways.question: Which A205 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM654.920 (C220d facelift) — modern diesel efficiency, reliable, and the most common engine in the UK parc.

problems.title: OM651 Rear Timing Chain (Pre‑Facelift Diesel)
problems.recommendation: On any pre‑facelift OM651 A205 over 80,000 miles with no documented chain history, budget for a chain inspection — £1,500–£2,500 now is far cheaper than the £2,800–£5,500 replacement that follows if the chain snaps. The facelift OM654 is a significant improvement.

problems.title: M274 Timing Chain & Fuel Pump (Pre‑Facelift Petrol)
problems.recommendation: On any pre‑facelift M274 A205, check for documented timing chain and fuel pump history — £800–£1,800 now is far cheaper than the £2,200–£5,500 replacement that follows a failure.

problems.title: M264 Valve & Cooling System (Facelift Petrol)
problems.recommendation: The M264 is generally more reliable than the M274, but valve and cooling system issues are emerging. Regular cooling system checks are recommended on facelift A205s over 60,000 miles.

problems.title: A205 Cabriolet Roof Mechanism & Rear‑Window Seal
problems.recommendation: Test the roof mechanism thoroughly before buying any A205 — a £400–£800 repair is far cheaper than the £1,500–£3,000 replacement that follows a complete mechanism failure. Check the rear window seal for signs of delamination.

replacementCosts.h2: A205 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Engine Page baseline — no Engine Page exists yet for any M274, M264, OM651, OM654 or M177 family. These costs are derived from the EM_Proprietary_Data Table A — Engine Replacement Economics for comparable engines (e.g., BMW B58B30, Mercedes OM642), adjusted for the A205's cabriolet‑specific labour access and marked [MB-QUOTE] accordingly. All figures are provisional pending dedicated Engine Pages.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are A205 two‑door cabriolet only. W205 saloon, S205 estate and C205 coupé share most of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages, since the cabriolet's additional structural bracing and roof mechanism affect weight, performance and labour access.
